name: Gradient Descent
key_facts: Gradient Descent is a first-order iterative optimization algorithm for finding a local minimum of a differentiable function
The update rule is: x_{n+1} = x_n - alpha * gradient(f(x_n)), where alpha is the learning rate
Variants include Stochastic Gradient Descent (SGD), Mini-batch GD, and Batch GD
Common extensions include momentum, Nesterov accelerated gradient, Adam, RMSProp, and AdaGrad
Convergence rate depends on the learning rate, function curvature, and conditioning
For convex functions, gradient descent converges to the global minimum
The learning rate is a critical hyperparameter that affects convergence speed and stability
forbidden_claims: Gradient Descent works on non-differentiable functions without modification
Gradient Descent always finds the global optimum for non-convex functions
Gradient Descent is a derivative-free optimization method
Gradient Descent was invented in the 21st century
